verdict_reason: If 2.44 were removed and only the chapter thesis plus 2.41 (resolved buddhi) plus 2.42-43 (the kāmya-vāda speakers and their trajectory) plus 2.45 (the operative imperative) remained, would the engineering analog be reconstructible? No. The 'attention-capture is the cognitive blocker; dropping not suppression is the fix' diagnostic requires precisely 2.44's apahṛta-cetas + samādhau na vidhīyate causal pair. Without 2.44, the chapter would name the speakers and the imperative but not the specific cognitive-impossibility consequence. The Tokio-debugger scenario hangs on this specific consequence-clause framing. PASS.
